question.text: If USD/INR moves from 82 to 83, which of the following is correct?

answer.text: USD has appreciated and INR has depreciated
INR has appreciated and USD has depreciated
Both USD and INR have appreciated
Neither currency has changed in value

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When USDINR increases from 82 to 83, it means 1 USD now buys more INR (83 instead of 82). Therefore, USD has strengthened/appreciated and INR has weakened/depreciated. **The Trap:** Students sometimes read the higher number as INR getting "more" and confuse it with INR appreciation. **Cross-Exam Context:** Currency appreciation/depreciation concepts are tested in all derivatives exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When the base currency buys MORE quotation currency, base currency has appreciated. When it buys LESS, it has depreciated.
